title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Athens, Georgia
twitter: Semi crash in Athens, GA? The truck's black box data could WIN your case — but trucking companies move FAST to protect it. Every hour counts under GA's 2-year limit. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) now. https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-athens-georgia
linkedin:
If your client was involved in a semi-truck crash in Athens, Georgia, one of the most critical — and time-sensitive — pieces of evidence is the truck's black box data. Electronic Logging Devices (ELDs) and Engine Control Modules (ECMs) record speed, braking, hours of service, and driver behavior in the moments before impact. This data can make or break a commercial truck accident claim.

The problem? Trucking companies deploy rapid response teams almost immediately after a crash. Their goal is to protect their interests — and that often means data gets overwritten, systems get reset, or preservation requests arrive too late.

Georgia's 2-year statute of limitations sounds like plenty of time. It isn't — not when it comes to black box data. Spoliation letters and preservation demands need to go out within days, not weeks.

WreckMatch connects injured victims and their families with attorneys from our 800+ law firm network who understand commercial trucking litigation in Georgia and know exactly how to move fast to secure this evidence.

If you're an attorney handling truck accident cases in Athens or anywhere in Georgia, or if you have a client who needs help right now, visit us at https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form to get connected today.
facebook:
If you or someone you love was hit by a semi-truck in Athens, Georgia, you may not realize that the truck itself was recording everything — speed, braking, driver hours, and more — right up until the moment of impact. That data lives inside the truck's black box, and it could be the most powerful evidence in your case.

Here's what too many families don't know: trucking companies move incredibly fast after a crash. Their teams are trained to respond quickly, and critical black box data can disappear before victims even know to ask for it. Georgia gives you 2 years to file a claim, but waiting even a few weeks to act could mean losing evidence forever.

You deserve answers, and you deserve an attorney who knows how to fight for them. WreckMatch connects truck accident victims with experienced lawyers across Georgia who know exactly how to preserve this evidence and hold trucking companies accountable.

Have you or someone you know been involved in a semi-truck accident and wondered what evidence might be out there waiting to be preserved?
reddit_title: Semi-truck crash in Georgia — how does black box data work and how do you make sure it doesn't disappear?
reddit_body:
After a semi-truck crash in Georgia, the truck's Electronic Control Module (ECM) and Electronic Logging Device (ELD) record critical data — vehicle speed, brake application, throttle position, hours of service, and more. This is often called the truck's "black box" and it can be the strongest evidence in a crash claim, showing exactly what the driver and truck were doing in the seconds before impact.

The most important thing to understand is that this data doesn't last forever. Trucking companies have rapid response legal teams that mobilize almost immediately after a serious crash. If a spoliation letter demanding data preservation isn't sent quickly, that information can be overwritten or lost. Georgia has a 2-year statute of limitations for injury claims, but waiting on black box evidence is a serious mistake — the window to preserve it is often measured in days.

If you're in this situation, your best move is to consult with a truck accident attorney as soon as possible — one who has experience with commercial trucking cases specifically, not just general personal injury. Sites like wreckmatch.com help connect crash victims with attorneys who handle exactly these kinds of cases and know how to act fast to lock down evidence before it's gone.
